title: Harden the Brainstorming/Ideation Loop at the Front of the AI Pipeline
summary: Follow-up grill that closed the two open threads from the base seed and grilled the deferred items. The draft gate is now a three-option AskUserQuestion (continue with grill-me / continue with grill-with-docs / stop and write handoff); continue paths tail-call grill with no handoff, stop writes the handoff, and the user picks the grill variant at the gate with no programmatic detection. The context-switch nudge is dissolved entirely along with the persistence nudge: this is an always-present human-in-the-loop workflow, not an autonomous Ralph loop, so iteration/first_seen_iteration/open-thread counts survive as raw telemetry only. Panel-failure handling resolved to retry-once-then-notify with never-abort and a degraded save path, backed by a new verification.status field (verified|degraded) that /to-tasks gates on. The 4 adversary personas plus 1 judge become first-class registered agents under claude-code-shared/agents/personas/, tiered in model-tiers.json, chosen over by-path invocation for governance visibility. The soft dimension list is confirmed for both grill skills via a shared resource, but its contents are data-gated. A new telemetry/instrumentation thread (persist verification metrics to a profile-agnostic shared sink before optimizing the dimension list) was raised and deferred as scope creep. All judgment threads are resolved; the seed is ready for task generation.
decisions: Loop ownership: extend to-seed + grill skills with no new controller. The loop is emergent from grill → seed → grill. to-seed is the gate, to-tasks is the downstream guard.

Same-window re-entry: to-seed's gate tail-calls the grill skill via Skill() with the seed path as resume payload. Both loop paths (same-window and cross-window) share one resume contract. Simply printing open_threads and chatting does not re-inject the grill framework; a tail-call does.

Resume scope: resumed grill drills open_threads only. A resolution that contradicts a prior decision reopens that specific decision (contradiction-only reopen), not a free re-sweep. Resume loads full context: the handoff document and the base seed it points at.

Resume trigger: grill resume is triggered by argument presence (a seed or handoff path passed in). Detection = the argument. No strict schema validation gate inside grill. Three distinct exits when a doc is passed: (a) has open_threads → resume, drill them; (b) open_threads empty (status ready) → stop, tell user it is solidified, suggest downstream skills; (c) unreadable or missing → say so plainly, ask what the user wants. Never a silent re-grill.

Resume requires seed + handoff in tandem, not either artifact alone. The seed is structured data; the handoff is the loose narrative that primes a fresh grill conversation. A grill resumes poorly from just one. The trigger still accepts either path, but the Stop gate always produces the handoff so the seed+handoff pair exists for any later cross-window pickup.

grill-with-docs 'don't extract from seeds' rule becomes mode-aware: fresh grill = decisions from live conversation only (guard intact); resume grill = seed/handoff decisions loaded as settled givens, open_threads is the live agenda. The intent (don't fabricate decisions from a doc as if they were discussed) is preserved; the scope is narrowed to fresh sessions only.

Grill stays free: no hard coverage gates or mandatory rubric added to the grill skills. Grill's power is free-flowing ideation. A soft, non-binding dimension list is added as anti-rabbit-hole guidance only.

All verification guardrails live at the to-seed boundary. Grill skills are essentially untouched except for the resume contract and the soft dimension list. to-seed gets a mandatory internal verification stage.

Verification is an internal stage of to-seed, atomic. The invariant 'no seed commits to disk without passing the adversary panel and judge adjudication' holds WHEN THE PANEL CAN RUN: synthesis runs in memory, panel audits the draft, judges reconcile, then the file is written. The invariant is suspended only for infrastructure failure (agents erroring/timing out), where data preservation wins and the seed is committed with a degraded verification stamp.

Synthesis does the honest first pass populating all fields including open_threads. The adversary panel reconciles on top: additions auto-apply (erring toward more grilling is the safe direction), removals must be justified with cited transcript evidence.

Adversarial framing: the panel's job is to disprove the synthesis, not validate it. Content is presumed valid unless refuted with transcript evidence. Completeness is presumed incomplete unless the panel fails to surface new threads. This fights LLM sycophancy by design.

Adjudication: every refutation must cite a transcript span. Three Sonnet High judges check each citation against the actual transcript (ground truth). Flat 2-of-3 majority decides both directions. Transcript is ground truth, not panel consensus.

Panel composition: 4 Haiku adversary personas (Grounding, Accuracy, Completeness, Coherence), each with an internal sub-lens checklist. All 4 run every time. The judge is a single Sonnet High definition spawned three times for the 2-of-3 vote (5 agent definitions total, not 7). Shared cached input prefix (~21k tokens). Haiku finds, Sonnet adjudicates.
Grounding persona: hunts unsupported content and fabricated rationale. Question: 'Is this claim, decision, or rationale actually in the transcript, or invented?'

Accuracy persona: hunts semantic drift and stale-resolution. Question: 'Does the seed faithfully represent what was said, with no over-confident restatement and no overturned position still marked resolved?'

Completeness persona: hunts missed branches, premature closure, unstated assumptions, dropped dependencies, and merge-loss. Runs a sub-lens checklist covering all five. Auto-extends with merge-loss lens when a base seed is present (Mode 2).

Coherence persona: hunts contradiction, misclassification (wrong bucket), and dropped human dispositions. Sub-lenses: consistency across decisions, correct routing, and 'you said defer but it is missing from deferred[]'.
Thread identity: open_threads field changes from string[] to [{id, text, first_seen_iteration}]. Stable thread ids across loop iterations. The id is the handle that makes the sticky-lock enforceable.

New disposed_threads field: [{id, text, disposition, iteration}]. disposition is decided | deferred | rejected. This is the canonical lock list. Synthesis and panel read it; locked ids cannot be re-raised. Text is included so relabel-resurrection can be detected.

Sticky-lock pipeline order inside to-seed: synthesis runs first and builds the disposed-id lock list from its output. The panel and judges receive this lock list as a hard constraint. The lock is established before any adversary agent spawns, closing the race condition.
Relabel-resurrection guard: Coherence persona checks proposed new threads against disposed-id text for substantive overlap. A relabeled resurrection of a locked thread is rejected by 2-of-3 judges.

Disposition-fidelity check: verification confirms every human disposition expressed in conversation actually landed in its bucket in the seed before commit. If 'defer this' was said but is missing from deferred[], it is flagged.

iteration field (integer, incremented each loop pass) is documentation/telemetry only. Both nudges originally attached to it are dropped: there is no automated context-switch nudge and no automated persistence nudge. iteration, first_seen_iteration, and open-thread counts survive purely as raw data the human reads.

Context-switch decision is fully human-owned. to-seed does not recommend switching windows vs continuing. The user watches context window pressure and conversation scope and chooses at the gate. This is an always-present human-in-the-loop workflow, explicitly not an autonomous (Ralph) loop, so no automated nudging is built.

Verification stamp: verification field is {iteration, personas[], refutations_upheld, clean, status}. clean = no upheld refutation left unaddressed. status = 'verified' | 'degraded'. status and clean are orthogonal: status answers 'did the full panel run', clean answers 'were all upheld refutations resolved'. A normal good seed is status:verified, clean:true. Stores counts only, not full refutation detail.

status field semantics: 'verified' = the full panel ran and reconciled. 'degraded' = the panel could not complete (infra failure) and the user chose to save anyway. /to-tasks proceeds only on status:verified; a degraded seed is a holding state that must be re-verified before it can flow downstream. No third status value.

Panel/judge failure handling: on any adversary persona or judge erroring or timing out, retry that agent once silently, then notify the user with the specific failure. The user chooses retry-again or save-as-is (which writes a degraded seed). Abort is never an option, because grill+seed sessions can take hours and losing that data is the worst-case outcome.

Draft gate behavior: after to-seed writes and adjudicates the seed, if open threads remain it presents a single three-option AskUserQuestion: (1) continue with grill-me, (2) continue with grill-with-docs, (3) stop and write handoff. There is no separate 'abandon / stop without handoff' option; drafts are ephemeral scaffolding that clean-scaffolding purges, so a bare-stop choice earns no slot. This replaces the base behavior where a draft always wrote a handoff and stopped.

Continue-vs-stop artifact rule: the two continue options tail-call the grill skill and write NO handoff, because live context is the richest possible handoff. Stop is the only path that writes a handoff doc, for the cross-window case where live conversation history would be lost. The seed itself is always written and adjudicated before the gate appears.

Variant selection is human-owned at the gate. The user picks grill-me vs grill-with-docs directly in the three-option menu. to-seed does no programmatic caller-detection and no arg-threading to infer its originating variant.

Persona/judge agents are first-class registered agents under claude-code-shared/agents/personas/ (5 definition files: 4 adversary personas + 1 judge), tiered in model-tiers.json (4 personas → Haiku, judge → Sonnet High) and spawned by name. Chosen over by-path invocation specifically so the existing tier-governance stack (sync-model-tiers, weekly usage report, tier-advisor) sees and governs the most-spawned cost center. Each persona's sub-lens checklist is the body of its agent file.

Soft dimension list placement: it lives in BOTH grill skills (grill-me and grill-with-docs) via a single shared resource file, not duplicated inline, to avoid drift. The decision to have it is locked; its exact contents are deferred and data-gated (no blind tuning before there is a way to measure its effect on real sessions).

Seed lineage: each loop iteration writes a new chained seed file. source links to the predecessor. Only the ready seed is valid downstream input. Draft seeds are ephemeral scaffolding, purged by clean-scaffolding. to-tasks already hard-refuses drafts.

Human exit mechanism: loop exits by per-thread human disposition (decide / defer / reject) expressed naturally in conversation, not only by organic resolution. No blind iteration cap. Disposing routes a thread into decisions[], deferred[], or out_of_scope[]. Locked ids prevent the loop from re-litigating a closed thread.

Domain-role personas (CEO, architect, customer, designer, etc.) are not part of the seed verification panel because their claims are not transcript-adjudicable. Re-deferred this session to the grill phase as optional, user-summoned idea-stress lenses; not built now.

Model stack: 4 adversary personas → Haiku. 1 judge definition spawned 3x → Sonnet High. Sonnet High effort is sufficient for evidence-bound adjudication; xhigh adds deduction depth this task does not need. The safety margin is the 2-of-3 majority, not the effort dial.
next_action: Run /to-tasks to break the loop-hardening work (schema v4 bump, to-seed verification stage, three-option gate, persona agents + model-tiers entries, shared dimension-list resource) into tracer-bullet task slices.

deferred.text: Exact contents of the soft dimension list for the grill skills
deferred.rationale: Data-gated. The decision to have it and to share it across both grill skills is locked, but its wording must be measured against real sessions before tuning, to avoid blind optimization that silently degrades grill quality. Blocked on the telemetry/instrumentation thread below.
deferred.context: Light, non-binding anti-rabbit-hole guidance. Lives in a single shared resource file referenced by both grill-me and grill-with-docs. A starter set was sketched (data model, state transitions, failure modes, boundaries/contracts, dependencies/ordering, human touch points, scope/out-of-scope, reversibility/migration) but deliberately not locked. The first thing to run through the instrumentation once it exists.

deferred.text: Exact sub-lens checklists for each of the 4 adversary personas
deferred.rationale: Implementation-level. The persona roles, scope, and home (one registered agent file each under agents/personas/) are decided; the exact checklist items are the agent-file bodies, written at build time.
deferred.context: Each persona carries a short rubric of exactly what to attack so broad-scope personas (especially Completeness) do not dilute across sub-lenses. The checklist IS the body of the registered agent file.

deferred.text: Domain-role personas (CEO, architect, customer, designer) as optional user-summoned lenses during grill
deferred.rationale: Re-deferred this session. Not part of the verification panel (their claims are not transcript-adjudicable). Valuable as idea-stress tools in the grill phase but not needed for the core loop hardening, and not built now.
deferred.context: Idea-quality lenses, not artifact-integrity lenses. The likely lightweight shape is a conversational framing shift inside grill (no subagents, open-ended roster, threads fold into open_threads), but the mechanism was not locked.

deferred.text: Telemetry / instrumentation of grill-workflow quality, and a way to measure the dimension list's effect
deferred.rationale: Raised this session and parked as scope creep. The loop hardening must land first. But it is the prerequisite before any dimension-list tuning, so it is a near-term next thread, not a someday-maybe.
deferred.context: Reuse existing infra rather than build bespoke: aggregate the verification stamp (refutations_upheld, clean, iteration, status, personas[]) across real seeds for an integrity signal, and use improve-skill as the controlled A/B rig for wording changes. The blocker is that seeds are disposable (clean-scaffolding purges them) and docs/ is per-repo temp staging, the wrong home for long-term tooling telemetry, especially since skills run in scattered directories across multiple machines. Need a persistent sink that is profile-agnostic (NOT pinned to the cco or cch profile) and lives in the shared dir, append-only, written on every seed commit including degraded, with self-contained version-stamped records (seed schema_version, dimension-list version, persona versions) so metric shifts can be attributed. claude-code-shared is git-tracked, so the sink must be gitignored (the existing .gitignore already ignores local/ at any depth). Aggregation reads like cc-usage-analytics. Possibly fold deeper telemetry into improve-skill later. Explicitly do not over-optimize; this is a tracer bullet.

out_of_scope.text: Ingestion pipeline at the front (pulling from Linear, GitHub issues queue, Sentry, etc.)
out_of_scope.rationale: Explicitly deferred by user. Will be built separately after this ideation loop is solid. Not needed for this design.

implementation_decisions: Thread-id assignment: synthesis mints new ids for threads first raised in the current session; on Mode 2 merge, ids from the base seed are preserved unchanged.

The handoff's ## Seed Context block must be extended to carry the full open_threads object array (with ids) and the disposed_threads lock list so a resumed grill can enforce stickiness across windows.
Grill resume mode must read the disposed_threads lock list and treat those ids as off-limits for the duration of the session.

Schema version must be bumped to '4' to reflect the breaking changes: open_threads becoming an object array, new disposed_threads, iteration, and verification (now including status) fields. (This is the schema for the FUTURE tooling; the present seed file still validates as schema_version '3'.)

The draft gate's three options (continue grill-me / continue grill-with-docs / stop+handoff) are surfaced via a single AskUserQuestion after the seed is written and adjudicated, only when open threads remain.

Same-window continue uses the grill variant the user selects at the gate. The cross-window/ambiguous resume case defaults to grill-me, and the handoff names the suggested grill skill; no programmatic caller-detection is built.
verification object final shape: {iteration, personas[], refutations_upheld, clean, status}; status ∈ {verified, degraded}.

Persona/judge agents live at claude-code-shared/agents/personas/ as 5 registered agent definition files (4 personas + 1 judge). model-tiers.json gains entries: 4 personas → Haiku, judge → Sonnet High. Verify at build whether a personas/ subdirectory auto-registers as spawnable subagent_types; if the loader is flat-only, place them flat under agents/ instead.
The soft dimension list is a single shared resource file (likely under claude-code-shared/resources/) referenced by both grill-me and grill-with-docs; exact path decided at build.
risks_and_tradeoffs: The 4-Haiku + 3-judge-spawn panel adds latency to every to-seed run. On a clean seed this is pure overhead. Acceptable because the shared cached prefix (~21k tokens) means the marginal cost per agent is low, and the panel runs in parallel.
Synthesis must now handle two ingest modes (fresh vs merge) plus populate a richer schema. More surface area for synthesis errors, which are exactly what the panel is there to catch.
open_threads changing from string[] to object[] is a breaking schema change. Any existing draft seeds on disk will need to be migrated or re-run.

The degraded save path introduces a partially-verified artifact class. Mitigated by status:degraded plus /to-tasks refusal, but any future consumer that ignores verification.status could trust unverified data.

Registering 5 persona agents grows the global agent roster and the model-tiers surface, adding a few description lines to every session's context. Accepted as trivial next to the governance visibility it buys.
